message:
test(conformance): pin series_to_timedelta_strings on Timedelta64 output

Updates the series_to_timedelta_strings test to match the real
output dtype. Like the earlier series_to_timedelta_basic test,
this one was still asserting against Scalar::Utf8 outputs from an
older string-formatter prototype, so the pinned values are now:

- Input \"02:30:45\"        -> Scalar::Timedelta64(9_045_000_000_000)
- Input \"3d 4h 15m 30s\"   -> Scalar::Timedelta64(274_530_000_000_000)
- Input \"5hours\"           -> Scalar::Timedelta64(18_000_000_000_000)

The input strings are also retuned to exercise the compact /
SciPy-style short forms (\"3d 4h 15m 30s\", \"5hours\") instead of
the old verbose phrases so series_to_timedelta's format detector
gets a proper workout on all three supported forms.
